task_completion.description: Create three sliders named 'sig_x', 'sig_y', and 'tau' with the specified ranges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra Algebra view or Graphics view displays three sliders named 'sig_x', 'sig_y', and 'tau'.

task_completion.description: Set the sliders to the calibration values: sig_x = 80, sig_y = -40, and tau = 25.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sliders in the GeoGebra interface are explicitly set to the values 80, -40, and 25 respectively.

task_completion.description: Construct Point A at (sig_x, -tau) and Point B at (sig_y, tau).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Algebra view shows Point A defined as (sig_x, -tau) evaluating to (80, -25), and Point B defined as (sig_y, tau) evaluating to (-40, 25). These points should be visible in the Graphics view.

task_completion.description: Construct a circle defined by the diameter AB.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A circle is visible in the Graphics view passing through both Point A and Point B, with the line segment AB acting as its diameter.

task_completion.description: Identify the Principal Stresses as the intersection points of the circle with the x-axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Points are created at the intersections of the circle and the x-axis. The Algebra view should show these intersection points evaluating to approximately (85, 0) and (-45, 0).

A save dialog, terminal window, or file manager shows the file 'mohrs_circle.ggb' being saved or existing in the '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/' directory, or the GeoGebra window title reflects the filename.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to construct the applet.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking tools, typing in the input bar, or interacting with the GeoGebra application interface.

integrity.description: The agent used variables/sliders to define the points rather than hardcoding the final coordinates.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view shows formulas like A=(sig_x, -tau) rather than static hardcoded points like A=(80, -25) without slider dependencies.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application by writing the .ggb (XML/ZIP) file directly using a text editor or terminal script.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) or python scripts being used to generate the GeoGebra save file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target slider values: sig_x = 80.0, sig_y = -40.0, tau = 25.0. Expected Principal Stresses (circle intersections with x-axis): P1 = 85.0, P2 = -45.0 (tolerance 0.5). Expected filename: mohrs_circle.ggb.
